plays.title: Correspondence
plays.description: A desperate web of ink and sealing wax carries a fugitive's survival across borders.

plays.title: Danton's Death
plays.description: A weary revolutionary is crushed by the relentless machinery of historical virtue.

plays.title: Hugo Translations
plays.description: A refugee translates French dramas in feverish night work to fund his escape.

plays.title: Lenz
plays.description: A poet flees into the cold mountains of madness, seeking an anchor for his mind.

plays.title: Leonce and Lena
plays.description: Two bored royal children flee their crowns, only to marry as wooden automata.

plays.title: Lucretia Borgia
plays.description: A soldier unknowingly stabs his mother, the infamous duchess, to avenge his poisoned friends.

plays.title: Maria Tudor
plays.description: A queen sets a deadly trap of high treason and execution for her unfaithful lover.

plays.title: On Cranial Nerves
plays.description: Galvanic current makes a dead fish twitch, proving life to be a biological machine.

plays.title: Aretino (The Chimney)
plays.description: A lost masterpiece of Renaissance satire rises as ashes from the fiancée's chimney.

plays.title: The Hessian Courier
plays.description: A revolutionary pamphlet uses raw tax statistics to ignite a peasant uprising.

plays.title: Woyzeck
plays.description: A simple soldier breaks from commands, hunger, and a knife.

plays.title: Die Spiegelgasse (The Zurich Exile)
plays.description: A materialistic doctor dissects the nerves of the barbel, while typhus claims his body.
